data.title: Nutrien upgraded at Morgan Stanley as potash market tightens
data.description:
Morgan Stanley on Wednesday upgraded Nutrien (NTR) to Overweight from a prior investing rating of Equal-weight, arguing that global potash markets are entering a “tighter for longer” phase that should support higher prices, earnings growth and valuation multiples through at least 2028.

In a January 14 report, analyst Vincent Andrews raised his price target on Nutrien (NTR) to $77 from $70, citing stronger-than-expected potash fundamentals and a revised supply-and-demand outlook that challenges the historical pattern of post-recovery demand slowdowns.

Fourth straight year of shipment growth

Morgan Stanley now expects global potash shipments to grow again in 2026, marking a fourth consecutive year of expansion. That hasn’t occurred since 2005, before the agriculture demand surge tied to emerging markets and biofuels.

The firm’s proprietary nutrient removal model suggests that the sharp decline in potash shipments during the peak of the Russia-Ukraine conflict in 2022 depleted soil nutrient banks globally. Despite a rebound in shipments since then, Morgan Stanley believes most recent volumes have merely offset annual nutrient removal rather than rebuilding inventories.

As a result, the bank sees limited risk of the typical “destocking” or soil-mining cycle that historically follows multiple years of shipment growth, absent a major supply or price shock.

Potash prices revised higher for key regions

Morgan Stanley now forecasts moderate potash price increases in 2026 compared with 2025, reversing its prior expectation for a modest decline. The firm raised benchmark price assumptions for the U.S., Brazil, Southeast Asia, China and India, while leaving European pricing largely unchanged due to regulatory and geopolitical uncertainty.

A key driver is capacity utilization. The bank expects global effective potash capacity utilization to remain above 90% through at least 2028, a level historically associated with firm pricing.

Supply growth is projected at just 2.8% annually from 2026 to 2028, with major greenfield projects, including BHP’s (BHP) Jansen mine, ramping more slowly than originally planned. The delay of Jansen’s startup into 2027 removes a potential inventory overhang and reinforces Morgan Stanley’s view that new potash capacity tends to arrive later and more gradually than markets expect.

Brazil and Southeast Asia underpin demand

Brazil remains a central pillar of Morgan Stanley’s constructive outlook. Potash inventories in the country are at five-year lows, while corn and soybean production continues to expand, increasing nutrient removal from soils. Although tighter farm credit conditions may delay purchases, the firm believes shipment growth in 2026 is difficult to avoid given depleted inventories.

In Southeast Asia, potash demand is expected to remain resilient despite softer crude palm oil prices. Morgan Stanley points to tight palm oil inventories, supportive biofuel policy in Indonesia and relatively favorable fertilizer affordability as reasons for steady to modestly rising potash consumption.

China stable, Europe more uncertain

Chinese potash shipments are expected to remain flat in 2026. Elevated prices are likely to prevent a meaningful rebuild of strategic reserves, while domestic production continues a gradual decline, reinforcing China’s long-term reliance on imports.

Europe, by contrast, remains the region of greatest uncertainty. Morgan Stanley flags risks tied to potential changes in sanctions on Belarus, Russian exports to the EU, and cost pressures from carbon border adjustment rules affecting fertilizer affordability. Importantly, these risks have limited direct impact on Nutrien (NTR), which does not serve the European potash market.

Why Nutrien stands out

Morgan Stanley estimates that higher potash prices lift its 2026 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization forecast for Nutrien (NTR) to roughly 12% above consensus. Potash and retail operations account for the majority of the company’s earnings, insulating it from risks facing nitrogen and phosphate producers.

The firm also sees room for valuation multiple expansion. Potash equities have recovered from trough levels, but still trade below pre-2022 norms. If prices and shipments prove more durable than the market expects, Morgan Stanley believes potash multiples could re-rate further.

“With higher earnings visibility and fewer execution risks than peers,” the report said, “Nutrien offers the most attractive risk-reward profile in fertilizers heading into 2026.”

data.title: Fed's Kashkari says consumer, AI capex boom keep economy resilient
data.description:
Overall, the economy remains "resilient," Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari said Wednesday in a fireside chat at a virtual event with the Wisconsin Bankers Association. He added that elevated borrowing costs to reduce inflation are not hampering growth as much as he had expected, owing to a solid consumer and the AI capex boom. Meanwhile, inflation (particularly goods and non-housing services) is still too high, and the labor market is gradually cooling.

Asked whether tariffs could lead to persistent inflation, the Minneapolis Fed chief noted slower pass-through to consumers, as businesses continued to work down inventory. However, he said that this month "could be a natural moment where businesses would then reset their pricing for the next year. So it may be that see a price bump as it relates to tariffs."

This is a developing story. Check back for updates.

data.title: Tesla offering discounts to clear out excess inventory of Indian Model Y SUVs
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is offering discounts of as much as $2,200 on its Model Y SUV in India in an effort to clear out roughly a third of the vehicles remaining unsold in the country.

The company shipped ~300 of the electric SUV into India this year but found that import tariffs, lackluster demand, coupled with cheaper and better-equipped models from BYD (BYDDF) (BYDDY) and BMW (BMWKY) left as many as 100 cars languishing at dealerships. Many of the unsold China-made Model Ys had previously been reserved, though the transactions were never completed, Bloomberg reported.

At issue is the price of the Model Y versus competitors. To protect the domestic car industry and limit luxury imports, India imposes a 110% tariff on imported vehicles, pricing the Tesla Model Y in the luxury category with a sticker price of ~$68,000 (~$80,000 for the long-range model).

By comparison, BYD’s (BYDDF) (BYDDY) comparable SUV, the Sealion 7, not only offers a more luxurious interior, but is priced at a much more competitive ~$57,000. Accordingly, BYD (BYDDF) (BYDDY) has enjoyed an 88% surge in registrations for its vehicles in India last year.   

Despite the difficulty selling a more expensive vehicle in the country and government efforts to encourage local manufacturing—including an offer of a lowered 15% tariff on carmakers who commit to investing in manufacturing within a three-year timeframe—Tesla (TSLA) has no immediate plans to make any vehicles in India and has opted instead for a “retail-first” strategy.

data.title: Alphabet further strengthens Gemini, giving it access to other Google apps
data.description:
Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google took additional steps to further strengthen its Gemini artificial intelligence tools on Wednesday, giving it access to users other Google apps, including YouTube and Gmail.

Known as Personal Intelligence, the measure aims to “personalize” Gemini to make it “uniquely helpful,” the company said in a blog post. “It connects Gmail, Photos, YouTube and Search in a single tap, and we’ve designed the setup to be simple and secure,” the company added.

“Personal Intelligence combines two core strengths: reasoning across complex sources and retrieving specific details, e.g from an email or photo, to provide uniquely tailored answers,” Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai said in post on X. “It’s built with privacy at the center. You choose exactly which apps to connect, these connected app settings are off by default.”

Personal Intelligence is available to Google AI Pro and AI Ultra subscribers in the U.S. in beta. It is available on the web, Android, and iOS.

The addition of allowing Gemini to have access to additional Google services provides it with an advantage to OpenAI's (OPENAI) ChatGPT. OpenAI has taken some steps to boost the usage and features available in ChatGPT in recent memory, including the launch of ChatGPT Health.

Earlier this week, Google and Apple (AAPL) co-announced a deal that will see Gemini help power the new, updated version of Siri, set to be released later this year. The two tech titans also said there will be “new experiences” for users as part of the partnership.

data.title: Atlanta Fed's Bostic still sees need to keep policy restrictive
data.description:
Atlanta Fed President Raphael Bostic believes that the Federal Reserve should keep its policy rate restrictive, he said on Wednesday.

"We haven't been at target on inflation for many, many years now,"  he said in a moderated conversation at the Atlanta Business Chronicle 2026 Economic Outlook. "We've made great progress, but we still have a distance to go."

Keeping the Fed's interest elevated is the central bank's tool for bringing down inflation. In December, the Federal Open Market Committee lowered its benchmark rate to 3.50%-3.75%, responding to a cooler labor market.

"I think we still need to have a restrictive policy and let things play out," he said. That's due to the expectation that the U.S. economy is expected to pick up, putting upward pressure on prices.

"The inflation challenge is not won yet, and we need to do everything we can" to bring it to target, said Bostic, who is not a voting member on the FOMC this year and who is retiring from the Atlanta Fed in February.

The Fed policymakers must focus on which of its mandates — price stability or maximum employment — is most at risk.  "Labor markets have weakened, but does 'weakening' equal 'weak'?," he asked. In the historical context, a 4.4% unemployment rate is still a solid labor market, he noted.

When asked about his response to the Department of Justice investigating Fed Chair Jerome Powell, Bostic said that Powell's video said "all it needed to say. I have nothing to add."

data.title: Tegna seesaws amid report that FCC Chair may lift local TV cap
data.description:
Tegna (TGNA) quickly rose 3.5%, though is now unchanged amid a report that FCC Chairman Brendan Carr could soon lift the agency's cap on local TV stations. 

Carr could lift the cap within one to three months, according to a Dow Jones report on Wednesday, which cited a research note from Benchmark analyst Daniel Kurnos. Removing the cap would allow for Nexstar Media's (NXST) proposed acquisition of Tegna (TGNA).

"We remain of the opinion that, despite all the noise in the ecosystem, either by cap raise or by waiver, Nexstar will successfully acquire TEGNA, creating massive synergies and an in-market juggernaut," Kurnos wrote. 

Carr has said he's in favor of getting rid of the rules that limit TV stations from owning outlets that reach more than 39% of U.S. households. President Donald Trump in late November appeared to indicate he was opposed to changing the TV ownership cap. 

Nextsar edged higher by 0.6%.

data.title: Mercury General announces dual listing of shares
data.description:
Mercury General (MCY) on Wednesday announced a dual listing of its common stock on NYSE Texas in addition to NYSE, effective January 15, 2026.

The Los Angeles-based multiple-line insurance organization will trade with the same MCY ticker symbol on the NYSE Texas.

"We have a significant business footprint in the Lone Star State, with more than 7% of our total direct written premiums generated in Texas in the past couple of years," said CEO Gabriel Tirador.

"This dual listing reaffirms our support for Texas' business-friendly capital market infrastructure and reinforces our long-standing relationship with the NYSE, while also enhancing shareholder value," added Tirador.

data.title: ClearBridge Large Cap Growth Strategy Q4: Adds Arista Networks, exits Starbucks, shifts restaurant exposure to Chipotle
data.description:
• The ClearBridge Large Cap Growth Strategy lagged the Russell 1000 Growth Index during a major market rally, mainly because it held fewer of the biggest AI stocks and avoided lower-quality AI-related companies.
  • It opened a position in Arista Networks (ANET), citing the company’s relatively small share in the enterprise segment and its strong potential to gain ground as organizations modernize their campus networking infrastructure.
  • It added that it will continue to be active in adding to positions or initiating new ones during pullbacks, such as it did with Palo Alto Networks (PANW) following its announcement of the CyberArk acquisition and Netflix (NFLX) when the stock sold off following disclosure of its intent to purchase Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD).
  • The strategy exited Accenture (ACN) in Q3 and sold Starbucks (SBUX) to focus its restaurant exposure on Chipotle (CMG).
     
  • Source: Press Relase

data.title: Solid Bio rises as Duchenne study gets fully enrolled
data.description:
Solid Biosciences (SLDB) traded higher on Wednesday after the gene therapy developer announced full enrollment in a Phase 1/2 study for its lead candidate SGT-003 in children with a muscle-wasting disorder called Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

The trial named INSPIRE DUCHENNE was previously expected to enroll 50–60 patients across clinical trial sites in the U.S., Canada, Italy, and the U.K.

“We're going to be notifying the sites, hopefully, in the next month or 2 that we're basically not going to take any more recruitment because we filled every possible slot that's possible,” said CEO Alexander Cumbo during the company’s presentation at the JPMorgan Healthcare Conference on Tuesday.

Additionally, Solid Bio (SLDB) provided an interim update from the study noting that as of Jan. 9, SGT-003 has been generally well tolerated among 33 patients who received dosing, adding to a previous safety update that represented 23 patients as of Oct. 31, 2025.

Additional data from the study, as well as an update on the company’s interactions with the FDA regarding a Phase 3 confirmatory trial design for SGT-003, are expected in mid-2026.

data.title: Gevo awarded patent for ethanol-to-olefins process to produce renewable jet fuel
data.description:
Gevo (GEVO) +2.2% in Wednesday's trading after saying it was awarded a patent for its ethanol-to-olefins technology to produce renewable jet fuel and chemicals, potentially lowering operating costs by as much as 35% compared to current technologies.

Gevo (GEVO) said the patented process produces light olefins from ethanol and can convert those olefins into transportation fuels using commercially proven alcohol-to-jet technologies; olefins can be used to make cost-effective renewable chemical building blocks such as bio-propylene.

Gevo (GEVO) is developing ethanol-to-olefins technology with partners LG Chem to deploy a next-generation ETO process for renewable chemical applications and with Axens for fuel applications.

"We continue to build an intellectual property portfolio that we believe will put Gevo in a cost-leadership position on [alcohol-to-jet technologies] for years to come," Gevo (GEVO) President Paul Bloom said.
